system.benefit:
Whenever you encounter a domesticated Beast with a Friendly or Helpful Attitude toward you, you can spend a Force Point as a Full-Round Action to bond the Beast to you as a mount. A Bonded Mount shares an emphatic link with you, allowing you to sense its emotions as a Free Action.

When you Ride a Bonded Mount, your mount uses your Reflex Defense and Will Defense instead of its own. Additionally, if your mount has any special senses (such as Scent, Darkvision, or Low-Light Vision) that you do not possess, you gain the benefits of its special senses as long as you are riding that mount.
system.description:
Whenever you encounter a domesticated Beast with a Friendly or Helpful Attitude toward you, you can spend a Force Point as a Full-Round Action to bond the Beast to you as a mount. A Bonded Mount shares an emphatic link with you, allowing you to sense its emotions as a Free Action.

When you Ride a Bonded Mount, your mount uses your Reflex Defense and Will Defense instead of its own. Additionally, if your mount has any special senses (such as Scent, Darkvision, or Low-Light Vision) that you do not possess, you gain the benefits of its special senses as long as you are riding that mount.

system.benefit:
You can make a Use the Force check in place of a Persuasion check when attempting to change the Attitude of an undomesticated creature with an Intelligence score of 2 or less. Additionally, you do not take the normal -5 penalty on the check if the creature can't speak or understand your language.

This Talent is identical to the Dathomiri Witch Talent of the same name, and both are considered to be the same Talent for the purposes of satisfying prerequisites.

system.benefit:
You can use The Force to convince a small Beast to carry objects, deliver messages, or perform other minor tasks for you. If you are near a Beast that is at least Indifferent to you (whether this be a pet you bring with you or a Beast encountered in the wild), you can make a Use the Force check against the Beast's Will Defense as a Swift Action.

If your Skill Check equals or exceeds the Beast's Will Defense, the Beast performs one task for you from the following list:

Deliver an Object: The Beast attempts, to the best of its ability, to deliver one object from your person to another target within 30 squares of you.
Retrieve an Object: The Beast attempts, to the best of its ability, to retrieve one unattended object within 30 squares of it and its line of sight and bring it to you.
Manipulate a Small Object: The Beast attempts, to the best of its ability, to press a button, pull a lever, or otherwise perform some minor activation of an unattended item within 30 squares.

system.benefit: Whenever you encounter a Beast with an Unfriendly Attitude toward you, you automatically shift its Attitude to Indifferent (no Skill Check required).
system.description: Whenever you encounter a Beast with an Unfriendly Attitude toward you, you automatically shift its Attitude to Indifferent (no Skill Check required).

system.benefit:
As a Swift Action once per turn, you can make a Use the Force check to touch the mind of a Beast with an Indifferent or better Attitude toward you, provided it is within 12 squares of you and in your line of sight. When you do so, the Beast makes an immediate active Perception check, and you are considered to perceive everything the Beast does, including noticing targets, as though you had made the check.

Additionally, until the end of your turn, you are considered to have line of sight to anything the Beast has line of sight to.

Make Persuasion vs Will; target cannot attack anyone within 6 squares if you do not have cover.
Catch a Second Wind as a reaction instead of a swift action.
Once per round, shield an ally; attacks that would target the ally target you instead.
Once per day, move +5 steps up the condition track (not persistent conditions); may be taken multiple times.
When you aid another to suppress an attack, the enemy takes -5 on its attack instead of -2.
Catch a Second Wind one extra time per encounter.
Use Mind Trick on beasts with Intelligence 2 or less; they cannot perform or understand complex directions.
system.description: You may use Mind Trick on beasts with Intelligence 2 or lower.

system.benefit: Convert energy from Negate Energy to activate any power in your suite.
system.description: Convert energy damage you negate into the ability to activate a Force power.

system.benefit: Gain DR 10 for one minute.
system.description: Gain damage reduction 10 for one minute.

system.benefit: Remove a debilitating condition affecting you and return to normal, including moving up to the top of the condition track.
system.description: Remove debilitating conditions and move up the condition track.

system.benefit: Designate one Force power; remove another power to refresh the designated power; you move -1 step down the condition track for 1 minute.
system.description: Designate a Force power to replace another; you move -1 down the condition track for 1 minute.

system.benefit: DC 15 Use the Force check to regain one Force power.
system.description: Make a Use the Force check to regain one expended Force power.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, activate a Force talent that requires a Force Point without spending one.
system.description: Once per encounter, activate a Force talent that normally requires a Force Point without spending one.

system.benefit: When you use Second Wind, gain additional hit points: 1d6 per Force Point possessed (maximum 10d6).
system.description: When you use Second Wind, gain additional hit points per Force Point you possess.

system.benefit: Attempt to rebuke a Force power; if you fail, lessen its effect by one step; only works on powers with variable effects.
system.description: Rebuke a Force power; on a failed rebuke, reduce its effect.

system.benefit: Immune to all mind-affecting effects for 1 minute.
system.description: Gain immunity to all mind-affecting effects for 1 minute.

system.benefit: Negate forced movement when being moved against your will.
system.description: Negate forced movement against your will.

system.benefit: Once per turn, if your Will Defense is targeted, you may replace Will with a Use the Force check; you take -5 to Use the Force until the end of your next turn.
system.description: Replace Will Defense with a Use the Force check when targeted.

system.benefit: Once per encounter reroll one Wisdom, Intelligence, or Charisma-based skill check (except Use the Force).
system.description: Once per encounter, reroll a Wisdom-, Intelligence-, or Charisma-based skill check (except Use the Force).

system.benefit: When you and allies are not surprised, number of allies equal to your Charisma modifier benefit from Quick Draw.
system.description: When you and allies are not surprised, a number of allies equal to your Charisma modifier gain the benefits of Quick Draw.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, Persuasion vs Will within line of sight can prevent the target from attacking you; if over by 5, they also cannot attack your allies.
system.description: Once per encounter, Persuasion vs Will Defense; the target cannot attack you, and if exceeded by 5, cannot attack your allies.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, Persuasion in a 12-square cone vs Will forces targets to lose their swift action and a standard action; one target loses a standard action if failed.
system.description: Once per encounter, Persuasion vs Will Defense in a 12-square cone; target loses its swift action and cannot take full-round actions, and loses its standard action.

system.benefit: Make a Persuasion cone (6 squares) vs Will; targets lose their swift action on their next turn and cannot take a full-round action.
system.description: Make a Persuasion check vs Will Defense in a 6-square cone; the target loses its swift action on its next turn and cannot take full-round actions.

system.benefit: Once per encounter negate the effect of a single attack or skill check made vs your Will Defense.
system.description: Once per encounter, negate the effect of a single attack or skill check that targets your Will Defense.

system.benefit: Each time you are attacked, the opponent takes -2 to Will Defense until the end of their next turn.
system.description: Each time you are attacked, the attacker takes –2 to Will Defense until the end of your next turn.

system.benefit: On a critical hit, target cannot move on its next turn.
system.description: On a critical hit, the target cannot move on its next turn.

system.benefit: Increase critical range of heavy weapons by 1.
system.description: With heavy weapons, increase the weapon's critical threat range by 1.

system.benefit: Increase critical range of rifles by 1.
system.description: With rifles, increase the weapon's critical threat range by 1.

system.benefit: When you score a critical hit, make an additional attack against a target in range once per turn.
system.description: Once per turn, when you score a critical hit, make an additional attack against a target in range.

system.benefit: On a critical hit, move the opponent back 1 square if not grabbed or into an obstacle; effect applies within two size categories.
system.description: On a critical hit, push the target 1 square if it is not grabbed or grappled and no more than two size categories larger.

system.benefit: On a critical hit, target suffers -2 Reflex Defense until healed to full hit points.
system.description: On a critical hit, the target takes –2 Reflex Defense until healed to full hit points.

system.benefit: On a critical hit, target’s speed is halved until healed to full hit points.
system.description: On a critical hit, the target's speed is halved until healed to full hit points.

system.benefit: Take -5 to Will Defense to gain +2 attack.
system.description: Take -5 Will Defense to gain +2 attack.

system.prerequisites: Charisma 13, Power of the Dark Side
system.benefit: You and allies within 6 squares gain +1 to all Defense scores until end of encounter while you remain conscious.
system.description: You and allies within 6 squares gain +1 to all defenses while you remain conscious.

system.benefit: Increase your Dark Side Score by 1 to stop moving down the condition track.
system.description: Increase your Dark Side Score by 1 to prevent moving down the condition track.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, return one dark side power to your suite without spending a Force Point.
system.description: Once per encounter, return one Dark Side Force power to your suite without spending a Force Point.

system.benefit: By touch, make a check vs target's Will Defense to drain knowledge; gain trained status in a skill or a Skill Focus if you already have the skill; target moves down the condition track; you gain a Dark Side Point.
system.description: Drain knowledge by touch, gaining a trained skill or Skill Focus; target moves down the condition track.

system.benefit: When spending a Force Point on an attack roll, you may roll an additional bonus die and take the best result. Doing so increases your Dark Side Score by 1.
system.description: When spending a Force Point on an attack, reroll the die and increase Dark Side Score by 1.

system.prerequisites: Dark Presence, Power of the Dark Side
system.benefit: Gain +2 attack and +2 damage if an ally of equal or higher level dies or is reduced to 0 hp within line of sight.
system.description: If an ally of equal or higher level is reduced to 0 hit points, gain +2 attack and damage.

system.benefit: Once per day, use a swift action in place of a move or standard action to activate a Force power.
system.description: Once per day, activate a Force power as a swift action.

system.prerequisites: Dark Side Score equal to Wisdom Score
system.benefit: If your Dark Side Score equals your Wisdom, upon death you become a dark side spirit able to possess others or enter an object.
system.description: Upon death, become a Dark Side spirit able to possess creatures or objects.

system.benefit: When you roll a natural 20 with a damage-dealing Force power, do not regain powers for damage; target also takes half damage again at the start of your next turn.
system.description: On a natural 20 with a damaging Force power, do not regain the power and deal half damage next turn.

system.benefit: If you succeed on an attack against a Flanked opponent, or any target that is denied its Dexterity bonus to Reflex Defense, you may spend a Force Point as a Free Action to deal additional damage to the target equal to 1d6 per Class Level (maximum 10d6).
system.description: If you succeed on an attack against a Flanked opponent, or any target that is denied its Dexterity bonus to Reflex Defense, you may spend a Force Point as a Free Action to deal additional damage to the target equal to 1d6 per Class Level (maximum 10d6).

system.benefit:
You let your anger swell into a Rage. As a Swift Action, you may spend a Force Point to gain a +2 Rage bonus on melee attack rolls and melee damage rolls for a number of rounds equal to 5 + your Constitution modifier. At the end of this duration, you move -1 step along the Condition Track.

While Raging, you cannot use Skills that require patience and concentration, such as Mechanics, Stealth, or Use the Force.
system.description:
You let your anger swell into a Rage. As a Swift Action, you may spend a Force Point to gain a +2 Rage bonus on melee attack rolls and melee damage rolls for a number of rounds equal to 5 + your Constitution modifier. At the end of this duration, you move -1 step along the Condition Track.

While Raging, you cannot use Skills that require patience and concentration, such as Mechanics, Stealth, or Use the Force.

system.benefit: Whenever you score a Critical Hit, you may spend a Force Point to also reduce the target's Speed by half until they are fully healed (that is, restored to maximum Hit Points).
system.description: Whenever you score a Critical Hit, you may spend a Force Point to also reduce the target's Speed by half until they are fully healed (that is, restored to maximum Hit Points).

system.benefit:
You can spend a Force Point to imbue a Weapon or some other portable object with the Dark Side of The Force, creating a Talisman that grants you protection from the Light Side. Creating the Dark Side Talisman takes a Full-Round Action.

While you wear or carry the Talisman, you gain a +2 Force bonus to one of your Defenses (Reflex, Fortitude, or Will) against Force Powers with the [Light Side] descriptor.

You can only have one Dark Side Talisman active at a given time (though you can have both a Dark Side Talisman and a Force Talisman active at the same time), and if your Dark Side Talisman is destroyed, you cannot create another one for 24 hours.
system.description:
You can spend a Force Point to imbue a Weapon or some other portable object with the Dark Side of The Force, creating a Talisman that grants you protection from the Light Side. Creating the Dark Side Talisman takes a Full-Round Action.

While you wear or carry the Talisman, you gain a +2 Force bonus to one of your Defenses (Reflex, Fortitude, or Will) against Force Powers with the [Light Side] descriptor.

You can only have one Dark Side Talisman active at a given time (though you can have both a Dark Side Talisman and a Force Talisman active at the same time), and if your Dark Side Talisman is destroyed, you cannot create another one for 24 hours.

system.benefit:
Whenever you use a Force Power with the [Dark Side] descriptor, you may reroll your Use the Force check, but you must accept the result of the reroll, even if it is worse.

Upon choosing this Talent, you can no longer use Force Powers with the [Light Side] descriptor.
system.description:
Whenever you use a Force Power with the [Dark Side] descriptor, you may reroll your Use the Force check, but you must accept the result of the reroll, even if it is worse.

Upon choosing this Talent, you can no longer use Force Powers with the [Light Side] descriptor.

system.benefit:
You can spend a Force Point to imbue a Weapon or some other portable object with the Dark Side of The Force, creating a Talisman that grants you protection from the Light Side. Creating the Greater Dark Side Talisman takes a Full-Round Action.

While you wear or carry the Talisman, you gain a +2 Force bonus to all of your Defenses against Force Powers with the [Light Side] descriptor.

You can only have one Greater Dark Side Talisman, or Dark Side Talisman, active at a given time (though you can have both a Greater Dark Side Talisman and a Greater Force Talisman active at the same time), and if your Greater Dark Side Talisman is destroyed, you cannot create another one for 24 hours.
system.description:
You can spend a Force Point to imbue a Weapon or some other portable object with the Dark Side of The Force, creating a Talisman that grants you protection from the Light Side. Creating the Greater Dark Side Talisman takes a Full-Round Action.

While you wear or carry the Talisman, you gain a +2 Force bonus to all of your Defenses against Force Powers with the [Light Side] descriptor.

You can only have one Greater Dark Side Talisman, or Dark Side Talisman, active at a given time (though you can have both a Greater Dark Side Talisman and a Greater Force Talisman active at the same time), and if your Greater Dark Side Talisman is destroyed, you cannot create another one for 24 hours.

system.benefit: If you are not surprised, you may forgo your standard action so non-surprised allies in line of sight gain an extra move or may reroll Initiative and keep the better result.
system.description: If you hit an opponent that has not yet acted in combat, deal +2 dice of damage.

system.benefit: Make Persuasion vs Will Defense to impose -2 to all of the target’s defenses until the end of your next turn.
system.description: Make a Persuasion check vs Will Defense to impose -2 to all defenses until the end of your next turn.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, allies in line of sight gain +4 flanking bonus to melee attacks.
system.description: Once per encounter, allies in line of sight gain a +4 flanking bonus to melee attacks.

system.benefit: Once per turn, make Persuasion vs Will Defense against all opponents in line of sight; the chosen target cannot attack you or one ally within 6 squares.
system.description: Once per turn, a Persuasion check prevents enemies in line of sight from attacking you or one ally.

system.benefit: Each once per encounter after you attack: if you use the same attack, you may attack as a reaction; or an opponent cannot attack you; or gain +2 attack if they haven't attacked.
system.description: After you attack, choose one benefit affecting enemy attacks or your own attack rolls.

system.benefit: +5 Will Defense vs checks to read your emotions; when you feint in combat, the target is flat-footed against you.
system.description: Gain +5 Will Defense against checks to read your emotions; feinted enemies are flat-footed.

system.benefit: As a Reaction, you can spend a Force Point to negate a Critical Hit scored against you, taking normal damage instead. You can spend this Force Point even if you have already spent a Force Point earlier in the round.
system.description: As a Reaction, you can spend a Force Point to negate a Critical Hit scored against you, taking normal damage instead. You can spend this Force Point even if you have already spent a Force Point earlier in the round.

swse.description: Your Will Defense now functions in place of Fortitude Defense

system.benefit: You gain a +1 bonus on melee attack rolls with Lightsabers. This bonus stacks with the bonus granted by the Weapon Focus (Lightsabers) feat.
system.description: You gain a +1 bonus on melee attack rolls with Lightsabers. This bonus stacks with the bonus granted by the Weapon Focus (Lightsabers) feat.

system.benefit: You gain a +2 bonus on melee damage rolls with Lightsabers. This bonus stacks with the bonus granted by the Weapon Specialization (Lightsabers) Talent.
system.description: You gain a +2 bonus on melee damage rolls with Lightsabers. This bonus stacks with the bonus granted by the Weapon Specialization (Lightsabers) Talent.

system.benefit: As a Standard Action, spend a Force Point to throw your Lightsaber through a 6-square line. Make one ranged attack roll and compare it to each target's Reflex Defense; targets hit take normal lightsaber damage, and targets missed take half damage. This attack is an Area Attack. You can pull your Lightsaber back with a Swift Action and a DC 20 Use the Force check.
system.description: As a Standard Action, spend a Force Point to throw your Lightsaber through a 6-square line. Make one ranged attack roll and compare it to each target's Reflex Defense; targets hit take normal lightsaber damage, and targets missed take half damage. This attack is an Area Attack. You can pull your Lightsaber back with a Swift Action and a DC 20 Use the Force check.

system.benefit: Once per turn, when you successfully Redirect an attack using the Redirect Shot Talent, you do not count the Deflect Talent use that triggered the redirected attack; subsequent Deflect attempts before your next turn impose penalties as normal.
system.description: Once per turn, when you successfully Redirect an attack using the Redirect Shot Talent, you do not count the Deflect Talent use that triggered the redirected attack; subsequent Deflect attempts before your next turn impose penalties as normal.

system.benefit: When you successfully make a Riposte attack using the Riposte Talent, you do not count the Block Talent use that triggered the Riposte; subsequent Block attempts before your next turn impose penalties as normal.
system.description: When you successfully make a Riposte attack using the Riposte Talent, you do not count the Block Talent use that triggered the Riposte; subsequent Block attempts before your next turn impose penalties as normal.

system.benefit: Once per encounter as a Swift Action, you can return any one spent Force Power with the [Lightsaber Form] descriptor to your Force Power Suite without spending a Force Point. You can select this Talent multiple times; each time you select it, you can use it one additional time per encounter.
system.description: Once per encounter as a Swift Action, you can return any one spent Force Power with the [Lightsaber Form] descriptor to your Force Power Suite without spending a Force Point. You can select this Talent multiple times; each time you select it, you can use it one additional time per encounter.

system.benefit: Whenever you make multiple attacks with any type of Lightsaber as part of a Full Attack, you reduce the penalty on your attack rolls by 2. You can take this Talent multiple times; each time you take this Talent, you reduce the penalty on your attack rolls by an additional 2.
system.description: Whenever you make multiple attacks with any type of Lightsaber as part of a Full Attack, you reduce the penalty on your attack rolls by 2. You can take this Talent multiple times; each time you take this Talent, you reduce the penalty on your attack rolls by an additional 2.

system.benefit: When lightsaber damage would kill your target by equaling or exceeding both its current Hit Points and Damage Threshold, you may instead deal half damage, move the target -1 step on the Condition Track, and sever one arm or leg. The injury creates a Persistent Condition that can only be removed by Surgery; a cybernetic prosthesis negates the reductions and penalties.
system.description: When lightsaber damage would kill your target by equaling or exceeding both its current Hit Points and Damage Threshold, you may instead deal half damage, move the target -1 step on the Condition Track, and sever one arm or leg. Arm injuries prevent use of that hand and impose -5 on Strength/Dexterity checks and skills. Leg injuries knock the target Prone, halve Speed and carrying capacity, and impose the same -5 penalty. The injury is a Persistent Condition until Surgery or cybernetics resolves it.

system.benefit: When you wield both a One-Handed Lightsaber and a Light Lightsaber, typically a Shoto or Guard Shoto, you can consider the One-Handed Lightsaber to be a Light Weapon. Additionally, if you have the Lightsaber Defense Talent, you can activate it as a Free Action on your turn instead of a Swift Action while wielding both weapons.
system.description: When you wield both a One-Handed Lightsaber and a Light Lightsaber, typically a Shoto or Guard Shoto, you can consider the One-Handed Lightsaber to be a Light Weapon. Additionally, if you have the Lightsaber Defense Talent, you can activate it as a Free Action on your turn instead of a Swift Action while wielding both weapons.

system.benefit: Any target successfully struck by a Lightsaber you Throw moves at half Speed, rounded down, until the beginning of your next turn.
system.description: Any target successfully struck by a Lightsaber you Throw moves at half Speed, rounded down, until the beginning of your next turn.

system.benefit: Brace an autofire weapon as a swift action when near cover to gain bracing benefits.
system.description: Brace an autofire weapon as a swift action if adjacent to an object that provides cover.

system.benefit: On a successful ram as a pilot, you take half the ram damage; if target is same size or smaller, it cannot move next round.
system.description: On a successful ram, you take half damage; if the target is the same size or smaller, it cannot move next round.

system.benefit: With a stun weapon, gain +1 attack and +1 damage die (nonlethal).
system.description: When using a stun weapon, gain +1 attack and +1 die of damage.

system.benefit: Not restricted to a straight line when running; may reroll Endurance checks.
system.description: You are not restricted to a straight line when running and may reroll Endurance checks.

system.benefit: Automatically improve the attitude of an indifferent NPC to friendly with no check required.
system.description: Automatically improve an indifferent character's attitude to friendly without a skill check.

system.benefit: When you move a target -1 down the condition track, their speed is halved until the condition is removed.
system.description: When you move a target down the condition track, their speed is halved until the condition is removed.

system.benefit: When you deal damage at the end of a charge, you knock the target prone if no more than one size larger than you.
system.description: When you deal damage at the end of a charge, knock the target prone if it is no more than one size larger than you.

system.benefit: Against a vehicle two sizes bigger that must be adjacent, make an opposed Pilot check; if you win, your attack vs that target +2 and their attacks vs you -2.
system.description: When adjacent to a vehicle two sizes larger, make an opposed Pilot check; you gain +2 attack and the target takes –2 attack.

system.benefit: Against a colossal size or smaller vehicle, a successful Pilot check turns a critical hit into a normal hit.
system.description: When piloting a Colossal or smaller vehicle, a successful Pilot check turns a critical hit into a normal hit.

system.benefit: Opponents in a dogfight with you take a -10 penalty to attack.
system.description: On an opposed Pilot check, opponents in a dogfight take –10 to attack rolls.

system.benefit: You may take 10 to increase speed; all-out movement is x5 instead of x4.
system.description: You may take 10 to increase speed; all-out movement increases speed by ×5 instead of ×4.

system.benefit: You are not required to move in a straight line when making an attack run.
system.description: You do not need to move in a straight line during an attack run.

system.benefit: When fighting defensively, may negate one additional attack per round with vehicular combat.
system.description: When fighting defensively, negate one additional attack per round with Vehicular Combat.

system.benefit: Once per encounter your vehicle avoids moving down the condition track.
system.description: Once per encounter, your vehicle avoids moving down the condition track.

system.benefit: You may roll twice for a Pilot check to initiate a dogfight and take the better result.
system.description: You may roll twice on Pilot checks to initiate a dogfight and keep the better result.

system.benefit: Allies within 6 squares can reroll one Pilot check and keep the best (once per ally).
system.description: Allies within 6 squares may reroll one Pilot check each and keep the better result.

system.benefit: Gain +2 attack with a selected vehicle type and may take 10 on Pilot checks for that vehicle.
system.description: Gain +2 attack with a selected vehicle type and may take 10 on Pilot checks with it.

system.benefit: If hit by an area attack while moving, take half or no damage.
system.description: If hit by an area attack while moving, take half or no damage.

system.benefit: As a swift action, assist an allied starfighter within 2 squares to grant +5 Pilot in a dogfight.
system.description: Assist an allied starfighter within 2 squares; ally gains +5 Pilot checks in dogfights.

system.benefit: Against an enemy you just hit with a melee attack, deal half damage and impose -5 attack against you until your next turn; usable once per round.
system.description: After hitting an enemy, they take half damage and -5 attack against you until next turn.

system.benefit: When you damage an opponent, you may attempt a Deception check to feint; if successful, one ally within 12 squares treats the target as flat-footed against your opponent.
system.description: After damaging an opponent, you may feint to make the target flat-footed against an ally.

system.benefit: Use Charisma modifier in place of Strength modifier with light melee weapons or a lightsaber.
system.description: You may use Charisma instead of Strength for attacks with light melee weapons or lightsabers.

system.prerequisites: Noble Fencing Style, base attack bonus +5
system.benefit: Once per encounter, make a single melee attack against an adjacent enemy who just damaged you.
system.description: Once per encounter, make a melee attack against an adjacent enemy who just damaged you.

system.prerequisites: Noble Fencing Style, base attack bonus +5
system.benefit: When you hit an opponent with a melee attack, you may deal half damage and switch positions with the foe; does not provoke attacks of opportunity.
system.description: You may deal half damage and switch places with the opponent without provoking attacks.

system.benefit: You are skilled at using a particular Force Power. Select one Force Power you know. When using that Force Power, you have the option of spending a Force Point to make two Use the Force checks, keeping the better result.

description.value:
You are skilled at using a particular Force Power. Select one Force Power you know. When using that Force Power, you have the option of spending a Force Point to make two Use the Force checks, keeping the better result.

This Talent may be selected multiple times. Its effects do not stack. Each time you select this Talent, you must choose a different Force Power.

combatActions.name: Force Power Adept
combatActions.description: You are skilled at using a particular Force Power. Select one Force Power you know. When using that Force Power, you have the option of spending a Force Point to make two Use the Force checks, keeping the better result.
combatActions.notes: Spend a Force Point while using the selected Force Power to make two Use the Force checks and keep the better result.
combatActions.label: Force Power Adept

system.benefit: You can make a Use the Force check in place of a Treat Injury check. You are considered Trained in the Treat Injury Skill. If you are entitled to a Treat Injury check reroll, you may reroll your Use the Force check instead (subject to the same circumstances and limitations).

description.value:
You can make a Use the Force check in place of a Treat Injury check. You are considered Trained in the Treat Injury Skill. If you are entitled to a Treat Injury check reroll, you may reroll your Use the Force check instead (subject to the same circumstances and limitations).

In addition, you can administer First Aid, Treat Disease, Treat Poison, and Treat Radiation without the requisite Medical Kit or Medpac.

system.effect: You can make a Use the Force check in place of a Treat Injury check. You are considered Trained in the Treat Injury Skill for purposes of using this Talent. If you are entitled to a Treat Injury check reroll, you can reroll your Use the Force check instead (subject to the same circumstances and limitations).

system.benefit: The Force shields you against ailments, toxins, and radiation poisoning, making you immune to Disease, Poison, and Radiation.

combatActions.description: The Force shields you against ailments, toxins, and radiation poisoning, making you immune to Disease, Poison, and Radiation.

combatActions.label: Fortified Body

system.benefit: Use the Force to reduce by 10 the effectiveness of Sense Surroundings attempts to detect you.
system.description: Enemies take –10 on Use the Force checks to detect you with Sense Surroundings.

system.benefit: When an enemy misses you with a lightsaber, you may move 2 squares without provoking attacks of opportunity.
system.description: When an enemy misses you with a lightsaber, you may move 2 squares without provoking attacks of opportunity.

system.benefit: When you aim, increase the difficulty for your attack to be deflected by +5.
system.description: When you aim, increase the difficulty for your attack to be deflected by +5.

system.benefit: If you resist a mind-affecting Force power, the user cannot use that same power against you again.
system.description: If you resist a mind-affecting Force power, the user cannot use that power against you again.

system.benefit: Add your class level to Will Defense against Use the Force effects.
system.description: Add your class level to Will Defense against Use the Force.

system.benefit: If you are moved by a Force power, reduce the movement distance by half.
system.description: When moved by a Force power, reduce the distance moved by half.

system.benefit: You may spend a Force Point to attune a melee weapon. Attuning the weapon takes a Full-Round Action. From that point forward, whenever you wield the attuned weapon, you gain a +1 Force bonus on attack rolls.

description.value:
You may spend a Force Point to attune a melee weapon. Attuning the weapon takes a Full-Round Action. From that point forward, whenever you wield the attuned weapon, you gain a +1 Force bonus on attack rolls.

The weapon is attuned to you alone; others who wield the weapon do not gain the Force bonus.

combatActions.name: Attune Weapon
combatActions.description: You may spend a Force Point to attune a melee weapon. Attuning the weapon takes a Full-Round Action. From that point forward, whenever you wield the attuned weapon, you gain a +1 Force bonus on attack rolls.
combatActions.notes: Spend a Force Point as a Full-Round Action to attune a melee weapon; attack roller reads the stored +1 Force attack bonus for that actor/weapon.

system.benefit: You may spend a Force Point to empower a melee weapon. Empowering the weapon takes a Full-Round Action. From that point forward, the Empowered Weapon deals an additional die of damage, but only when wielded by you.

description.value:
You may spend a Force Point to empower a melee weapon. Empowering the weapon takes a Full-Round Action. From that point forward, the Empowered Weapon deals an additional die of damage, but only when wielded by you.

For example, an empowered Lightsaber deals 3d8 points of damage, instead of 2d8 points of damage. Others who wield the weapon do not gain the bonus damage die.

combatActions.name: Empower Weapon
combatActions.description: You may spend a Force Point to empower a melee weapon. Empowering the weapon takes a Full-Round Action. From that point forward, the Empowered Weapon deals an additional die of damage, but only when wielded by you.

system.benefit: When you create a Force Talisman, you can select a single Force Power from your Force Power Suite. description.value: When you create a Force Talisman, you can select a single Force Power from your Force Power Suite. system.benefit: You may spend a Force Point to imbue a weapon or some other portable object with The Force, creating a Talisman that provides protection to you. Creating the Talisman takes a Full-Round Action.

description.value:
You may spend a Force Point to imbue a weapon or some other portable object with The Force, creating a Talisman that provides protection to you. Creating the Talisman takes a Full-Round Action.

While you wear or carry the Talisman on your person, you gain a +1 Force bonus to one of your Defenses (Reflex Defense, Fortitude Defense, or Will Defense).

You may only have one Force Talisman active at a given time, and if your Force Talisman is destroyed, you may not create another Force Talisman for 24 hours.

combatActions.name: Force Talisman
combatActions.description: You may spend a Force Point to imbue a weapon or some other portable object with The Force, creating a Talisman that provides protection to you. Creating the Talisman takes a Full-Round Action.
combatActions.notes: Manual/deferred talisman creation and equipment card. No always-on defense bonus is applied until talisman UI exists.
combatActions.label: Force Talisman

system.benefit: You can hurl Simple Weapons (Melee), or Advanced Melee Weapons your size or smaller as a Standard Action, treating it as a Thrown Weapon. The Thrown Weapon deals normal weapon damage if it hits.

description.value:
You can hurl Simple Weapons (Melee), or Advanced Melee Weapons your size or smaller as a Standard Action, treating it as a Thrown Weapon. The Thrown Weapon deals normal weapon damage if it hits.

If the weapon deals piercing or slashing damage, it becomes embedded in your target, remaining there and causing an additional die of damage each round at the end of the target's turn, and also when it is removed (removing the embedded weapon is a Swift Action, and an adjacent ally can remove the embedded weapon for you).

Your target must be within 6 squares of you. The weapon does not automatically return to you, but you can retrieve it with the Move Object Force Power (dealing an additional die of damage in the process, if the weapon is embedded in the target, as above).

combatActions.name: Force Throw
combatActions.description: You can hurl Simple Weapons (Melee), or Advanced Melee Weapons your size or smaller as a Standard Action, treating it as a Thrown Weapon. The Thrown Weapon deals normal weapon damage if it hits.
combatActions.notes: Throw an Empowered Simple or Advanced melee weapon as a Standard Action against a target within 6 squares.
combatActions.label: Force Throw

system.benefit: As a Reaction, you may negate a melee attack by making a successful Use the Force check. The DC of the Use the Force check is equal to the result of the attack roll you wish to negate, and you take a cumulative -5 penalty on your Use the Force checks to use this Talent for every time you have used Primitive Block since the beginning of your last turn.

description.value:
As a Reaction, you may negate a melee attack by making a successful Use the Force check. The DC of the Use the Force check is equal to the result of the attack roll you wish to negate, and you take a cumulative -5 penalty on your Use the Force checks to use this Talent for every time you have used Primitive Block since the beginning of your last turn.

You must have an Empowered Weapon drawn to use this Talent, and you must be aware of the attack and not Flat-Footed. You may spend a Force Point to use this Talent to negate an attack against an adjacent character.

You may use the Primitive Block Talent to negate melee Area Attacks, such as those made by the Whirlwind Attack feat. If you succeed on the Use the Force check, you take half damage if the attack hit, and no damage if the attack missed.

system.benefit: Prerequisite: Manifest Guardian Spirit
system.description:
Prerequisite: Manifest Guardian Spirit

When you have a manifested Guardian Spirit, the Guardian Spirit also discourages your enemies and distracts them from their goals. Any enemy within 3 squares of your Guardian Spirit takes a -2 penalty to their Will Defense and a -2 penalty on attack rolls against you.

system.benefit: You have a Guardian Spirit watching over you from the realm of The Force, providing you with insight and advice. Your Guardian Spirit might be an old mentor or an ancient member of your Force Tradition who has chosen to guide your Destiny.
system.description:
You have a Guardian Spirit watching over you from the realm of The Force, providing you with insight and advice. Your Guardian Spirit might be an old mentor or an ancient member of your Force Tradition who has chosen to guide your Destiny.

When you use the Search Your Feelings application of the Use the Force Skill, you can instead choose to consult your Guardian Spirit. When you do so, you learn more than just whether the results of your actions will be favorable or unfavorable; you also learn the nature of any immediate consequences, including potential encounters, and whether or not certain actions will bring you closer to achieving your Destiny.

Additionally, you gain one bonus Force Point each day (available after you rest for at least 6 hours). This bonus Force Point can only be used to improve a Force Power or activate a Force Technique or a Force Secret. If you do not spend your bonus Force Point in a given day, it is lost at the start of the next day.

system.benefit: You can spend a Force Point as a Swift Action to have your Guardian Spirit manifest for the duration of the encounter. Your Guardian Spirit appears in any square within 6 squares of you. A Guardian Spirit occupies a space, but creatures can move through the Guardian Spirit without obstruction.
system.description:
You can spend a Force Point as a Swift Action to have your Guardian Spirit manifest for the duration of the encounter. Your Guardian Spirit appears in any square within 6 squares of you. A Guardian Spirit occupies a space, but creatures can move through the Guardian Spirit without obstruction.

As long as the Guardian Spirit remains within 12 squares of you, you gain a +1 morale bonus on attack rolls, a +2 morale bonus on Use the Force checks, and a +2 morale bonus to your Will Defense. You can move the Guardian Spirit up to 6 squares as a Swift Action once per turn.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, your Guardian Spirit offers you Vital Encouragement, urging you to press on despite adversity. As a Free Action, you gain Bonus Hit Points equal to 10 + one-half your Heroic Level. Damage is subtracted from Bonus Hit Points first, and any Bonus Hit Points remaining at the end of the encounter are lost. Bonus Hit Points from multiple sources do not stack.

You can spend a Force Point as a Standard Action to create an aura of unsettling discomfort around you. You make a Use the Force check when you activate this Talent and compare the check result to the Will Defense of any creature that comes within 6 squares of you.

If your Use the Force check result equals or exceeds the creature's Will Defense, that target takes a -2 penalty on attack rolls and Skill Checks while within 6 squares of you. This aura lasts for the remainder of the encounter.
combatActions.notes: Spend a Force Point and roll Use the Force; selected targets that fail are flagged with the attack penalty. Range/LoS remains adjudicated.

system.benefit:
Whenever you build a Lightsaber (see Advanced Lightsaber Construction), you do so with such expertise that it makes the Weapon even more refined and elegant. When you build a Lightsaber, you can choose to add one extra Lightsaber Accessory or Modification at the time of creation.

And, when you hit a target with a Lightsaber that you built, you can always choose to reroll one damage die from your damage roll, but you must accept the result of the reroll, even if it is worse.

In addition, you can mentor another character while they construct their own Lightsaber. When you do so, you reduce the Use the Force check DC for Advanced Lightsaber Construction by -5.

system.benefit: As a Standard Action, weaken one opponent's resolve with Persuasion; on success, the target moves down the Condition Track. This is Mind-Affecting and deals no HP damage.
system.description:
As a Standard Action, you can weaken the resolve of one opponent with your words. The target must have an Intelligence of 3 or higher, and it must be able to see, hear, and understand you. Make a Persuasion check; if the result equals or exceeds the target's Will Defense, it moves -1 step along the Condition Track. The target gets a +5 bonus to its Will Defense if it is higher level than you.

If the target reaches the end of its Condition Track, it does not fall unconscious; instead, it cannot attack you or your allies for the remainder of the encounter unless you or one of your allies attacks it or one of its allies first. This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

You affect the Condition Track of the specific character (usually the Vehicle's Commander), not a Starship.

system.benefit: Use the Force vs Will Defense to expose a target, causing -2 Reflex Defense against you and allies who hear you until the end of your next turn.
system.description:
As a Standard Action, you can peer into The Force and search for weaknesses in the defenses of your enemies. Make a Use the Force check against the Will Defense of a target creature within 12 squares of you and in your line of sight.

If the Skill Check equals or exceeds the target's Will Defense, that target takes a -2 penalty to their Reflex Defense against you and all allies who can hear and understand you until the end of your next turn.

system.benefit: After damaging an opponent with a Lightsaber attack, Take 10 on Persuasion checks until the end of your next turn.
system.description:
Prerequisite: Adept Negotiator

Whenever you damage an opponent with a Lightsaber attack, you can Take 10 on any Persuasion checks you make before the end of your next turn, even if you would not normally be able to.

system.benefit: Once per turn as a Swift Action, remove one ongoing Mind-Affecting effect from one allied target in line of sight.
system.description: Once per turn as a Swift Action, you can remove one ongoing Mind-Affecting effect (such as the effects of Demand Surrender or Weaken Resolve Talents, or the effect of being moved to the end of the Condition Track by the Adept Negotiator Talent, or the ongoing effects of the Mind Trick Force Power) from a single allied target within line of sight.

system.benefit: Other nearby Force-users with Farseeing can Aid Another on your Farseeing-related Use the Force checks as a Reaction.
system.description:
Prerequisite: Farseeing

When you use Farseeing, or a Force Power or Talent that has Farseeing as a prerequisite, other Force-users with Farseeing in their Force Power Suite can Aid Another on your Use the Force check as a Reaction, if they are within 6 squares of you.

system.benefit: Once per round as a Swift Action, grant an ally Bonus Hit Points equal to 5 + your Charisma modifier; take -5 on UTF until your next turn.
system.description:
Once per round as a Swift Action, you grant one ally within 12 squares of you (and within your line of sight) Bonus Hit Points equal to 5 + your Charisma modifier.

These Bonus Hit Points last until the beginning of your next turn (at which point any remaining Bonus Hit Points are lost), and any damage dealt to that ally comes out of Bonus Hit Points first.

You take a -5 penalty on all Use the Force checks until the beginning of your next turn.

system.benefit: Once per encounter as a Swift Action, an ally adds your Wisdom bonus to Will Defense against Mind-Affecting effects.
system.description:
Prerequisite: Adept Negotiator

Once per encounter as a Swift Action, you can choose one ally within your line of sight that can hear and understand you. Until the end of the encounter, that ally adds your Wisdom bonus to its Will Defense against Mind-Affecting effects.

You must make the Skill Check before the end of your turn, or the benefit of the Aid Another Action is lost. An ally that has already used Aid Another to assist you since the end of your last turn may not be targeted by this Talent.

system.benefit: You gain a +2 insight bonus to your Will Defense. Also, as a Swift Action, you can spend a Force Point to give all allies within 6 squares of you a +2 insight bonus to their Will Defense that lasts for the remainder of the encounter. This bonus does not extend to allies outside the range of the effect, even if they move within 6 squares of you later on. Allies who benefit must remain within 6 squares of you to retain the bonus, and they lose it if you are knocked unconscious or killed. This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

description.value: You can use the Move Light Object application of the Use the Force skill to cast aside Grenades that are thrown at you. As a Reaction when you are attacked by a Grenade of any kind, you can make a Use the Force check with a DC equal to the attack roll of the incoming Grenade attack. If your check equals or exceeds the DC, you hurl the Grenade to a location where it explodes harmlessly, negating the attack. Whether or not you are successful, you take a -5 penalty on Use the Force checks until the start of your next turn.

If the designated opponent Moves or Withdraws before the beginning of your next turn, you can choose to move with that opponent, up to a total distance equal to your current speed. Unless your opponent uses the Withdraw Action or makes an Acrobatics check to avoid Attacks of Opportunity, its movement provokes an Attack of Opportunity from you for the first square moved as normal, but no subsequent squares in the same movement. If your target moves farther than your speed, you must still end this movement closer to the target than you began.

system.benefit:
Choose a Talent that you already possess. The Talent you select must be from the Lightsaber Combat Talent Tree, the Duelist Talent Tree, or the Lightsaber Forms Talent Tree. Once per day, as a Standard Action, you can spend a Force Point to impart the benefits of the chosen Talent to one or more allies, effectively granting them the Talent (even if they don't meet the prerequisites).

An ally must be within 12 squares of you, and must be able to see and hear you to gain the Talent; once gained, its benefits last until the end of the encounter.

You can share the Talent with a number of allies equal to one-half your Class Level (rounded down). Only allies who are Trained in the Use the Force Skill can gain the benefits of the Shared Talent.

You can take this Talent multiple times. Each time you do so, you must select a different Talent to share with this ability. You can share each Talent with your allies only once per day.

system.benefit:
As a Standard Action, you can spend any one use of a Force Power currently in your Force Power Suite, adding a use of that Force Power to the Force Power Suite of any ally Trained in the Use the Force skill. The ally must be within 12 squares of you and in your line of sight.

When your ally uses that Force Power, it disappears from his or her Force Power Suite. If the ally does not use the Force Power before the end of the encounter, it is permanently removed from his or her Force Power Suite.

system.benefit:
You can use the Farseeing Force Power on a location instead of on an individual creature, peering into the location's past to view events that occurred there. Unlike the normal use of the Farseeing Force Power, you are actually looking into the location's past (at a time you designate), and you must be standing in the location being viewed.

The target DC for your Use the Force check is 20, +1 for each day into the past that you are attempt to scry. When you look into the past, you need only specify a time in a number of days, as you can sense tremors in The Force that focus you visions on meaningful events that day.

You retain this bonus (and may not use this Talent again) until your target surrenders, is reduced to 0 Hit Points, moves to the bottom of the Condition Track, or until the encounter ends.

system.benefit:
You have access to a network of Jedi sympathizers. While in a civilized area, you can call upon this network of allies once per game session for one of the following purposes:

Acquire Equipment or Funds: You can use your contacts to obtain material that might otherwise be Licensed, Restricted, Military, or Illegal, provided the total value of the Equipment does not exceed your Jedi Knight Level x 500 credits. Alternatively, you can obtain a number of credits from your contacts equal to this amount to spend as you see fit.
Obtain Information: Your contacts provide you with information, automatically succeeding on a Gather Information check (and covering the credit cost of the check) provided the DC does not exceed 20.
Receive Medical Attention: Your contacts provide you and up to three of your allies with medical attention as dispensed by a skilled physician or healer. The length of the treatment cannot exceed 24 hours, but is otherwise free of charge and completely private.
Secure Safe House: One of your contacts provides a safe redoubt for you and up to three of your allies, for a number of days equal to your Jedi Knight Class Level. While in hiding, you have no contact with anyone other than the individual who is hiding you. Once the allotted time is up, you must leave the Safe House or risk discovery. For each day you remain in hiding past this deadline, the Gamemaster should roll 1d20. On a result of 15 or higher, your Safe House is discovered, and your contact's complicity in keeping you hidden is exposed.

Secure Safe House: One of your contacts provides a safe redoubt for you and up to three of your allies, for a number of days equal to your Jedi Knight Class Level. While in hiding, you have no contact with anyone other than the individual who is hiding you. Once the allotted time is up, you must leave the Safe House or risk discovery. For each day you remain in hiding past this deadline, the Gamemaster should roll 1d20. On a result of 15 or higher, your Safe House is discovered, and your contact's complicity in keeping you hidden is exposed.

system.benefit:
When you interact with another sentient creature, you can use a Swift Action to reduce the impression you leave on it. When you have finished interacting with the creature, you make a Use the Force check, and if the check result exceeds the target's Will Defense, it does not remember interacting with you once you are gone.

A target that is higher in level than you gains a +5 bonus to its Will Defense to resist this ability. This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

system.benefit:
You can spend a Force Point as a Standard Action to create a "Haze" that hides you and your allies from the perception of others. You can hide a number of creatures in line of sight equal to your Class Level. Make a Use the Force check and compare the result to the Will Defense of any opponent that moves into line of sight of any creature hidden by your Force Haze. If your check result equals or exceeds the opponent's Will Defense, all hidden creatures are treated as if they had Total Concealment against the opponent.

The Force Haze lasts for up to 1 minute but is dismissed instantly if anyone hidden by the Force Haze makes an attack.

You may use this Talent to hide a single Vehicle while you are on board. The Vehicle can't have a size penalty to Reflex Defense, Initiative, or Pilot checks greater than your Heroic Level. Thus, a Gargantuan Starship (size penalty of -5) can be hidden only by a character of 5th level or higher.
