system.description: Gain +2 to all defenses until the end of the encounter or until you attack.

system.benefit: When you roll a natural 20 on an attack against a foe with Dark Side Score 1+, you may activate any power with the light side descriptor.
system.description: On a natural 20 against a Dark Side opponent, activate a Light Side Force power.

system.benefit: Reroll an attack against an opponent with a Dark Side Score of 1+.
system.description: Reroll an attack against a creature with a Dark Side Score.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, return any light side power without spending a Force Point.
system.description: Once per encounter, regain a Light Side Force power without spending a Force Point.

system.benefit: As a Reaction, negate a melee attack by making a Use the Force check against the attack roll result. You take a cumulative -5 penalty for each Block or Deflect used since the beginning of your last turn. You may negate melee area attacks for half/no damage, and may spend a Force Point to protect an adjacent character. Requires a drawn and ignited lightsaber, awareness, and not being Flat-Footed.
system.description: Reaction: make a Use the Force check against a melee attack roll to negate it; cumulative Block/Deflect penalty applies.

system.benefit: You can use the Block Talent without a lightsaber while wearing a Cortosis Gauntlet. If you successfully Block an attack with a lightsaber while wearing a Cortosis Gauntlet, the attacking lightsaber is deactivated.
system.description: Use Block with a Cortosis Gauntlet; successful Blocks against lightsabers deactivate the attacking lightsaber.

system.benefit: As a Reaction, negate a ranged attack by making a Use the Force check against the attack roll result. You take a cumulative -5 penalty for each Block or Deflect used since the beginning of your last turn. You may mitigate autofire or Force Lightning-style barrages, and may spend a Force Point to protect an adjacent character. Requires a drawn and ignited lightsaber, awareness, and not being Flat-Footed. Cannot negate Colossal (Frigate)+ vehicle attacks unless point-defense.
system.description: Reaction: make a Use the Force check against a ranged attack roll to negate it; cumulative Block/Deflect penalty applies.

system.benefit: As a Swift Action, gain a +1 deflection bonus to Reflex Defense until the start of your next turn while your lightsaber is drawn and ignited. Multiple selections increase the bonus by +1 each, maximum +3. The bonus does not apply while Flat-Footed or unaware.
system.description: Swift Action: gain a temporary deflection bonus to Reflex Defense until the start of your next turn.

system.benefit: You can throw a lightsaber as a Standard Action, treating it as a thrown weapon rather than an improvised thrown weapon. If the target is within 6 squares, you can pull the lightsaber back to your hand as a Swift Action with a DC 20 Use the Force check.
system.description: Throw a lightsaber normally and optionally pull it back with a DC 20 Use the Force check if within 6 squares.

system.benefit: Whenever you successfully Redirect a blaster bolt and hit your target, the redirected attack deals +1 die of damage.
system.description: Triggered rider: successful Redirect Shot hits deal +1 damage die.

system.benefit: As a Standard Action, make a melee attack with a lightsaber against an adjacent opponent. If the attack hits, it deals normal damage and reduces the target's speed to 2 squares until the end of your next turn.
system.description: Standard Action lightsaber attack; on hit, target speed becomes 2 squares until the end of your next turn.

system.benefit: Once per round when you successfully Deflect a single blaster bolt, you can make an immediate ranged attack against another target in line of sight. Apply normal range penalties, not counting the distance the bolt traveled to reach you. On a hit, it deals normal weapon damage. Autofire barrages and other projectiles cannot be redirected.
system.description: Triggered by successful Deflect: once per round redirect a single blaster bolt as an immediate ranged attack.

system.benefit: As a Reaction once per encounter, make a lightsaber attack against a being whose non-area melee attack you successfully negated with Block. You cannot use Riposte when negating melee area attacks.
system.description: Once per encounter after successfully Blocking a non-area melee attack, make a lightsaber attack against the attacker.

system.benefit: Whenever you wield both a one-handed lightsaber and a short lightsaber or guard shoto, you gain a +2 competence bonus on attack rolls made with the short lightsaber or guard shoto.
system.description: Conditional +2 competence bonus on attacks with a short lightsaber or guard shoto while dual-wielding with a one-handed lightsaber.

system.benefit: You gain a +2 bonus on melee damage rolls with Lightsabers.
system.description: You gain a +2 bonus on melee damage rolls with Lightsabers.

system.benefit: You may add your Dexterity bonus (instead of your Strength bonus) on damage rolls when wielding a Lightsaber. When you wield a Lightsaber two-handed, you may apply double your Dexterity bonus (instead of double your Strength bonus) to the damage.
system.description: While Ataru is your active Lightsaber Form, you may add your Dexterity bonus instead of your Strength bonus on damage rolls when wielding a Lightsaber. When you wield a Lightsaber two-handed, you may apply double your Dexterity bonus instead of double your Strength bonus to the damage. Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time.

lightsaberFormImplementation.rule: Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time; inactive forms grant no benefits.

system.benefit: Once per round when an opponent hits you with a melee attack, you may spend a Force Point as a Reaction to make an immediate attack against that opponent.
system.description: While Djem So is your active Lightsaber Form, once per round when an opponent hits you with a melee attack, you may spend a Force Point as a Reaction to make an immediate attack against that opponent. Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time.

lightsaberFormImplementation.rule: Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time; inactive forms grant no benefits.

system.benefit: When you use the Lightsaber Defense Talent, you gain twice the normal deflection bonus to your Reflex Defense when you are wielding two Lightsabers.
system.description: While Jar'Kai is your active Lightsaber Form, when you use the Lightsaber Defense Talent, you gain twice the normal deflection bonus to your Reflex Defense when you are wielding two Lightsabers. Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time.

lightsaberFormImplementation.rule: Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time; inactive forms grant no benefits.

system.prerequisites: Weapon Focus (Lightsabers), Weapon Specialization (Lightsabers), Base Attack Bonus +10
system.benefit: Once per encounter, you may spend a Force Point as a Swift Action to designate a single opponent in your line of sight. For the remainder of the encounter, you may reroll your first attack roll each round against that opponent, keeping the better of the two results.
system.description: While Juyo is your active Lightsaber Form, once per encounter you may spend a Force Point as a Swift Action to designate a single opponent in your line of sight. For the remainder of the encounter, you may reroll your first attack roll each round against that opponent, keeping the better of the two results. Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time.

lightsaberFormImplementation.rule: Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time; inactive forms grant no benefits.

system.benefit: When wielding a single Lightsaber in one hand, the deflection bonus you gain from the Lightsaber Defense Talent increases by 2 (to a maximum of +5).
system.description: While Makashi is your active Lightsaber Form, when wielding a single Lightsaber in one hand, the deflection bonus you gain from the Lightsaber Defense Talent increases by 2, to a maximum of +5. Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time.

lightsaberFormImplementation.rule: Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time; inactive forms grant no benefits.

system.benefit: When wielding a Lightsaber, you gain a +1 bonus to your Reflex Defense and Will Defense.
system.description: While Niman is your active Lightsaber Form and you are wielding a Lightsaber, you gain a +1 bonus to your Reflex Defense and Will Defense. Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time.

lightsaberFormImplementation.rule: Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time; inactive forms grant no benefits.

system.benefit: Whenever you redirect a deflected blaster bolt (see the Redirect Shot Talent), you gain a +5 bonus on your ranged attack roll.
system.description: While Shien is your active Lightsaber Form, whenever you redirect a deflected blaster bolt with the Redirect Shot Talent, you gain a +5 bonus on your ranged attack roll. Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time.

lightsaberFormImplementation.rule: Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time; inactive forms grant no benefits.

system.benefit: When using the Block or Deflect Talents, you only take a -2 penalty on your Use the Force check for every previous Block or Deflect attempt since your last turn.
system.description: While Shii-Cho is your active Lightsaber Form, when using the Block or Deflect Talents, you only take a -2 penalty on your Use the Force check for every previous Block or Deflect attempt since your last turn. Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time.

lightsaberFormImplementation.rule: Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time; inactive forms grant no benefits.

system.benefit: You may Take 10 on Acrobatics checks to Tumble, even when distracted or threatened. Additionally, each threatened or occupied square that you Tumble through only counts as 1 square of movement.
system.description: While Sokan is your active Lightsaber Form, you may Take 10 on Acrobatics checks to Tumble, even when distracted or threatened. Additionally, each threatened or occupied square that you Tumble through only counts as 1 square of movement. Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time.

lightsaberFormImplementation.rule: Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time; inactive forms grant no benefits.

system.benefit: You may reroll a failed Use the Force check when using the Block or Deflect Talents.
system.description: While Soresu is your active Lightsaber Form, you may reroll a failed Use the Force check when using the Block or Deflect Talents. Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time.

lightsaberFormImplementation.rule: Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time; inactive forms grant no benefits.

system.prerequisites: Weapon Focus (Lightsabers), Weapon Specialization (Lightsabers), Base Attack Bonus +12
system.benefit: By harnessing the unique characteristics of a Lightsaber, you can catch your opponent off guard by quickly shutting off and reigniting the blade. When wielding a Lightsaber, you may spend two Swift Actions to make a Deception check to Feint in combat.
system.description: While Trakata is your active Lightsaber Form and you are wielding a Lightsaber, you may spend two Swift Actions to make a Deception check to Feint in combat by quickly shutting off and reigniting the blade. Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time.

lightsaberFormImplementation.rule: Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time; inactive forms grant no benefits.

system.prerequisites: Juyo, Weapon Focus (Lightsabers), Weapon Specialization (Lightsabers), Base Attack Bonus +12
system.benefit: When attacking with a Lightsaber, you score a critical hit on a natural roll of 19 or 20. However, a natural 19 is not considered an automatic hit; if you roll a natural 19 and still miss the target, you do not score a critical hit.
system.description: While Vaapad is your active Lightsaber Form, when attacking with a Lightsaber, you score a critical hit on a natural roll of 19 or 20. However, a natural 19 is not considered an automatic hit; if you roll a natural 19 and still miss the target, you do not score a critical hit. Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time.

lightsaberFormImplementation.rule: Only one Lightsaber Form can be active at a time; inactive forms grant no benefits.

system.benefit: Acquire equipment worth your character level × 1000 credits; reduce black market multiplier by 1.
system.description: Acquire equipment equal to character level × 1,000 credits and reduce black market cost.

system.benefit: You may make Knowledge checks untrained.
system.description: All Knowledge skills may be used untrained.

system.benefit: Trained in Mechanics; time to install new vehicle systems reduced by 25%.
system.description: Reduce time required to install vehicle systems by 25%.

system.benefit: Once per day, friends may take 20 on a skill check with modifier equal to 5 + half your heroic level; time taken is 10 × result minimum.
system.description: Once per day, allies may take 20 on skill checks using your modifier.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, take 20 on a Persuasion check with no increase in time required.
system.description: Once per encounter, take 20 on a Persuasion check without increasing time.

system.benefit: Once per day, make a skill check as if trained; may be used multiple times per day with separate skills.
system.description: Once per day, make a trained skill check even if untrained.

system.benefit: Each time you gain a level (including the level at which you select this Talent), you receive 5,000 credits for each level you have in a Lineage-eligible class (Noble and Corporate Agent). Each eligible level is granted only once.

description.value:
Each time you gain a level (including the level at which you select this Talent), you receive 5,000 credits for each level you have in a Lineage-eligible class (Noble and Corporate Agent). Each eligible level is granted only once.

The credits appear in a civilized, accessible location of your choice or in your private bank account.

system.benefit: Gain one follower with Armor Proficiency feat and trained in Perception; may be taken up to three times.
system.description: Gain a loyal follower trained in Perception.

system.benefit: When you attack: redirect the attack to your follower, move your follower toward the target, or impose -1 attack per follower on ranged attacks against the target.
system.description: Redirect attacks to followers and penalize enemy attacks.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, as a reaction when you are damaged by an attack or Force power, your follower may attack that opponent.
system.description: Once per encounter, a follower may attack a creature that damages you.

system.benefit: Followers gain the Toughness feat.
system.description: Your followers gain the Toughness feat.

system.benefit: If your attack exceeds the target’s Fortitude Defense, the target is poisoned; poison attacks each round with d20 + your heroic level vs Fortitude, deals 1d6 + half your heroic level damage, and moves the target down the condition track until it misses or is cured.
system.description: When your attack exceeds Fortitude Defense, you poison the target.

system.benefit: You can modify a poison’s delivery method with a Knowledge (life sciences) check; DC equals the Treat Injury DC.
system.description: You may alter poison delivery methods with a successful Knowledge check.

system.benefit: A poisoned target is denied Dexterity to Reflex Defense as long as they remain poisoned.
system.description: Poisoned targets are denied Dexterity to Reflex Defense.

system.benefit: Increase the Treat Injury DC to detect or treat the poison by 5.
system.description: Increase the Treat Injury DC to detect your poisons by 5.

system.benefit: Poisons you use gain +2 attack against the target’s Fortitude Defense.
system.description: Poisons gain a +2 bonus on attack rolls against Fortitude Defense.

system.benefit: Add your armor's Fortitude Defense bonus to Elite Trooper Damage Reduction; lightsabers that do not ignore DR still do not ignore this DR.
system.description: Add your armor's Fortitude Defense bonus to your damage reduction; lightsabers that normally ignore DR do not ignore yours.

system.benefit: Once per encounter on your turn, move your speed as a free action before taking other actions.
system.description: Once per encounter on your turn, move your speed as a free action before any other action.

system.benefit: Once per encounter when making more than one attack, add one damage die for each successful hit.
system.description: Once per encounter, when making more than one attack, add one extra die of damage for each successful hit.

system.benefit: Once per encounter when you reduce an opponent to 0 hp, gain +5 attack on your next attack in the same encounter.
system.description: Once per encounter, when you reduce an opponent to 0 hit points, gain +5 attack with your next attack in the same encounter.

system.benefit: If your damage exceeds a creature's DR, you ignore that DR for that attack.
system.description: If your unarmed damage exceeds damage reduction, ignore the damage reduction.

system.benefit: With Teras Kasi basics, unarmed attacks deal an additional damage die.
system.description: When making unarmed attacks, deal one additional die of damage.

system.prerequisites: Martial Arts I-III, Teras Kasi Basics
system.benefit: Only full-round unarmed actions allowed; take full attack as a standard action.
system.description: When making only unarmed attacks as a full-round action, you may take a full attack as a standard action.

system.benefit: If you parry a melee attack, immediately make an unarmed attack as a reaction against the attacker.
system.description: When you successfully parry a melee attack, make an immediate unarmed attack as a reaction.

system.prerequisites: Martial Arts I-II, Teras Kasi Basics
system.benefit: When fighting defensively, may negate a melee attack using parry; if successful and attack beats your melee attack, negate the attack and apply -2 cumulative attack vs others.
system.description: When fighting defensively, negate a melee attack with an opposed melee attack roll; cumulative –2 attack penalty against other targets.

system.benefit: Attract nonheroic minions with levels equal to 3/4 your level; may have multiple minions.
system.description: Attract a nonheroic minion of level equal to three-quarters your level; may be taken multiple times.

system.benefit: Once per turn, redirect an attack to an attracted minion.
system.description: Once per turn, redirect an attack to one of your attracted minions.

system.benefit: Once per turn, redirect an attack to an attracted minion; the minion gains Reflex Defense equal to half your class level.
system.description: Once per turn, redirect an attack to a minion; the minion gains Reflex Defense equal to half your class level.

system.benefit: Once per turn, redirect an attack to an attracted minion; the minion gains Reflex Defense equal to your class level and makes a free attack against the attacker.
system.description: Once per turn, redirect an attack to a minion; the minion gains Reflex Defense equal to your class level and makes a free attack against the attacker.

system.benefit: Designate an ally to move immediately at normal speed; must move immediately or the effect is wasted; usable three times per turn.
system.description: Up to three times per turn, an ally may immediately move their speed or lose the action.

system.benefit: Designated ally can make a standard or move action immediately or the effect is wasted.
system.description: An ally may immediately take a move or standard action or lose it.

system.benefit: Designated ally can take a standard and a move action immediately or the effect is wasted.
system.description: An ally may immediately take both a move and standard action or lose them.

system.benefit: Allies in line of sight gain +2 attack and +2 to skill checks against your designated target.
system.description: Allies in line of sight gain +2 attack and skill checks against your designated target.

system.benefit: Any cover bonus to your Reflex Defense is +2 when adjacent to a minion.
system.description: If adjacent to a minion, any cover bonus you receive is increased by +2 Reflex Defense.

system.benefit: Any cover bonus to your Reflex Defense is +2 when adjacent to a minion.
system.description: If adjacent to a minion, any cover bonus you receive is increased by +2 Reflex Defense.

system.benefit: All allies in line of sight and within 6 squares may withdraw as a swift action until the start of your next turn.
system.description: All allies in line of sight and within 6 squares may withdraw as a swift action until the start of your next turn.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, increase the speed of allies in line of sight by 2.
system.description: Once per encounter, increase the speed of allies in line of sight by 2 squares.

system.benefit: If a minion is killed, they are replaced by one of the same level after 24 hours.
system.description: If a minion is killed, it is replaced by another of the same level after 24 hours.

system.benefit: Gain +5 attack on attacks of opportunity made with melee weapons.
system.description: Gain +5 attack on melee attacks of opportunity.

system.benefit: Use Deception to feint as a two-swift action against an opponent you threaten.
system.description: Use Deception to feint as two swift actions against opponents you threaten.

system.prerequisites: Dual Weapon Mastery I, Weapon Finesse
system.benefit: With two light melee weapons or two lightsabers, when you make a full attack with one weapon you gain a free attack with the other.
system.description: When wielding two light melee weapons or lightsabers, gain a free attack with the second weapon during a full attack.

system.prerequisites: Dual Weapon Mastery II, Dual Weapon Flourish I, Master of Elegance
system.benefit: With two light melee weapons or two lightsabers, you may make a full attack as a standard action when using both weapons (once per turn).
system.description: Once per turn, make a full attack as a standard action when using two light melee weapons or lightsabers.

system.prerequisites: Dual or Single Weapon Flourish I
system.benefit: Add Dexterity to melee damage with light melee weapons; double Dexterity bonus if wielding two-handed.
system.description: Add Dexterity modifier to melee damage with light melee weapons; double if wielded two-handed.

system.benefit: Reduce multiattack penalty by 2 for advanced melee weapons.
system.description: Reduce the attack penalty for multiple attacks with advanced melee weapons by 2.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, after a successful feint you may make an attack with a light melee weapon or lightsaber.
system.description: Once per encounter, after a successful feint, make an immediate attack with a light melee weapon or lightsaber.

system.benefit: With one light melee weapon or lightsaber, you can move your speed as a free action.
system.description: When wielding a single light melee weapon or lightsaber, move your speed as a free action.

system.prerequisites: Master of Elegance, Single Weapon Flourish I
system.benefit: With one light melee weapon or lightsaber, you may make a full attack as a standard action (once per turn).
system.description: Once per turn, make a full attack as a standard action when wielding a single light melee weapon or lightsaber.

system.benefit: If you exceed Reflex Defense by 5 or more with a melee attack, deal +1 damage die per melee weapon group.
system.description: If you exceed Reflex Defense by 5 or more with a melee attack, deal +1 die of damage (per melee weapon group).

system.benefit: If adjacent or in the same square as a foe, gain +1 melee attack.
system.description: When adjacent to or sharing a square with an enemy, gain +1 melee attack.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, ignore a target’s armor or equipment bonuses with a melee attack.
system.description: Once per encounter, ignore armor or equipment bonuses with a melee attack.

system.benefit: If you move an opponent down the condition track with a melee attack, that target cannot take a standard or full-round action on its next turn.
system.description: If you move a target down the condition track with a melee attack, the target cannot take standard or full actions on its next turn.

system.benefit: When using Unrelenting Assault, every adjacent target takes damage equal to your Strength bonus with a melee weapon.
system.description: When using Unrelenting Assault, all adjacent targets take damage equal to your Strength bonus.

system.benefit: Deal +2 damage against targets damaged by an ally since the end of your last turn.
system.description: Designate a vehicle or target; batteries deal extra damage when using Tactical Fire.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, all enemies take -2 Will Defense; Persuasion becomes a class skill for you.
Once per encounter, if you damage an opponent, reduce the target’s damage threshold by 2 for the encounter.
When you reduce an enemy to 0 hit points, make Persuasion vs targets within 6 squares; those targets take -2 attack for the rest of the encounter (once only).
When you deal damage, gain +5 Will Defense until your next turn unless you are surprised or flat-footed.
Double your speed for 1 round; must wait 5 rounds between uses.
When affected by a debilitating condition, convert its penalty into a bonus for 1 round, then move down the condition track.
Deal +2 damage against targets damaged by an ally since the end of your last turn (stacks up to +10).
When you deal damage over the target’s threshold, gain +2 damage against that target for the rest of the encounter.
system.description: Once per encounter, when you reduce a foe to 0 hit points or the bottom of the track, gain a bonus standard action.

system.benefit: Ignore cover when firing or throwing a weapon with burst or splash.
system.description: Ignore cover when firing or throwing weapons with burst or splash effects.

system.benefit: Ignore door and wall thresholds when using mines and non-grenade explosives.
system.description: Ignore damage thresholds of doors and walls when using mines or non-grenade explosives.

system.benefit: Repair droids gain +1 hp for every point your Mechanics check exceeds DC 20.
system.description: When repairing droids, restore +1 hit point for each point your Mechanics check exceeds DC 20.

system.benefit: A non-grenade explosive you set can turn normal terrain into difficult and difficult into normal.
system.description: Use explosives to alter terrain between normal and difficult terrain.

system.benefit: Once per turn, one vehicle in your line of sight that can hear you ignores difficult terrain and gains +5 Pilot to avoid hazards.
system.description: Once per turn, a vehicle in line of sight may ignore difficult terrain and gain +5 Pilot to avoid hazards.

system.prerequisites: Tech Specialist, Repairs on the Fly
system.benefit: When creating a field-created weapon, add one modification at creation.
system.description: When creating a field weapon, add one Tech Specialist modification at creation.

system.benefit: Repair as a standard action once per day per droid, object, or vehicle.
system.description: Repair droids, objects, or vehicles as a standard action once per day per target.

system.benefit: Sabotage an object with an energy cell/power pack to turn it into a grenade; can be retemplates/apps/stored with a swift action.
system.description: Convert an object with an energy cell or power pack into a grenade.

system.benefit: Trained in Knowledge (technology); increase speed of a droid or vehicle by 1 square if you occupy or are adjacent to it (once per round).
system.description: Increase the speed of an adjacent droid or vehicle by 1 square once per round.

system.benefit: DC 15 Mechanics check to add hit points to a vehicle equal to 5 × class level.
system.description: With a DC 15 Mechanics check, restore hit points equal to 5 × your class level to a vehicle.

system.benefit: DC 15 Tactics check designate a single object or creature; allies who can hear you deal extra d6 damage until your next turn.
system.description: Designate a target; allies who can hear you deal +1d6 damage until your next turn.

system.benefit: DC 15 Tactics check: allies gain +1 attack vs flanked targets or +1 Defense vs attacks of opportunity next turn; synergizes with Born Leader/Battle Analysis for +2.
system.description: Allies gain +1 attack vs flanked enemies or +1 defense vs attacks of opportunity.

system.benefit: When you use Assault Tactics, the target takes a cumulative -1 Reflex each time it is damaged (max -5).
system.description: Targets take cumulative –1 Reflex Defense each time they are damaged (maximum –5).

system.benefit: Allies within 10 squares gain a +10 cover bonus if they are in cover until your next turn.
system.description: Allies in cover gain +10 cover bonus until your next turn.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, grant hit points equal to 5 + half your class level to all allies within 20 squares and line of sight.
system.description: Once per encounter, allies within 20 squares gain hit points equal to 5 + half your class level.

system.benefit: You may take half or all damage for an adjacent ally; an ally may take the same for you.
system.description: You or an ally may take half or all damage for an adjacent ally.

system.benefit: Enemies in line of sight lose competence/insight/morale/dodge bonuses to Reflex Defense; opposed check to resist.
system.description: Enemies lose morale, insight, dodge, and competence bonuses to Reflex Defense.

system.benefit: Redistribute defenses: -2 to one Defense to add +1 to another Defense.
system.description: Take –2 to one defense and gain +1 to another defense.

system.benefit: Redistribute defenses: -5 to one Defense to add +2 to another Defense.
system.description: Take –5 to one defense and gain +2 to another defense.

system.benefit: Redistribute defenses: -5 to two Defenses to add +5 to another Defense.
system.description: Take –5 to two defenses and gain +5 to another defense.

system.benefit: Use Assault, Deployment, or Field Tactics as a swift action instead of a standard action.
system.description: You may use Assault, Deployment, or Field Tactics as a swift action.

system.benefit: Once per day add half your officer class level to all Defenses for 1 round; must declare at start of your turn.
system.description: Once per day, add half your class level to all defenses for one round.

system.benefit: Make Deception vs Will Defense to move through a threatened area without provoking attacks of opportunity; movement through the area counts as 2 squares.
system.description: Use Deception vs Will Defense to move through threatened squares without provoking attacks.

system.benefit: Each once per encounter after you attack: if you damage the target, impose -5 Reflex; or reduce the target’s speed by 2; or against two opponents within 2 squares, each takes -5 attack but you roll damage once.
system.description: After you attack, impose penalties or bonuses based on positioning.

system.benefit: An attack against an opponent denied Dexterity to Defense moves them -1 step down the condition track.
system.description: Attacks against flat-footed enemies move them down the condition track.

system.benefit: Suppress morale and insight bonuses for all in line of sight until your next turn.
system.description: Suppress morale and insight bonuses to enemies in line of sight until your next turn.

system.benefit: Make Persuasion vs Will Defense within 12 squares; target’s speed is -2 and must spend a swift action with their standard action until their next turn.
system.description: Use Persuasion vs Will Defense to reduce an enemy's speed and actions.

system.benefit: If you move at least 2 squares and end in a different square, gain +1 to all defenses.
system.description: After moving and changing squares, gain +1 to all defenses.

system.benefit: If you fail a Persuasion check to change attitude, reroll using Deception.
system.description: If you fail a Persuasion check to change attitude, reroll using Deception.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, seize an object from an adjacent target with +10 to Disarm; must have a free hand and cannot conceal the object.
system.description: Once per encounter, seize an object from an adjacent target.

system.benefit: If you move at least 2 squares, gain +1 attack until your next turn.
system.description: If you move at least 2 squares, gain +1 attack until your next turn.

system.benefit: If the opponent is flat-footed or denied Dexterity to Defense, deal +1d6 damage within 6 squares (maximum 10d6).
system.description: Deal +1d6 damage against flat-footed or Dexterity-denied opponents.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, make Deception vs Will Defense against a target in line of sight; target must spend a swift action with their standard action to attack until your next turn.
system.description: Once per encounter, force a target to spend a swift action to attack.

system.benefit: Once per round, target within 12 squares and line of sight takes -5 to one skill until the start of your next turn.
system.description: Once per round, impose a -5 penalty to one skill check for a target within line of sight.

system.benefit: Deal sneak attack damage when you use Skirmisher.
system.description: Deal Sneak Attack damage when using Skirmisher.

system.benefit: Enemies in line of sight take -2 to defenses until your next turn.
system.description: Enemies in line of sight take -2 to all defenses until your next turn.

system.benefit: If you deal damage to an opponent denied Dexterity to Defense, you may impose -5 to attack and melee damage instead of moving them down the condition track.
system.description: Instead of moving an enemy down the condition track, impose attack and damage penalties.

rules.label: Esoteric Technique

description.value: Whenever you gain a level, you also gain a number of additional Force Points equal to the number of Force Talents you possess (maximum +6).

combatActions.description: Whenever you gain a level, you also gain a number of additional Force Points equal to the number of Force Talents you possess (maximum +6).
combatActions.notes: Progression/reconciliation reminder; applies at level gain, capped at +6.

rules.label: Mystic Mastery
rules.summary: Whenever you gain a level, you also gain a number of additional Force Points equal to the number of Force Talents you possess (maximum +6).

system.benefit: You gain a +5 Force bonus on Skill Checks made to perform a Force Regimen.

description.value: You gain a +5 Force bonus on Skill Checks made to perform a Force Regimen.

combatActions.notes: Force Regimen performance checks gain +5 Force bonus. Force Regimen UI remains the authority for selected regimens.

rules.label: Regimen Mastery
rules.summary: You gain a +5 Force bonus on Skill Checks made to perform a Force Regimen.

system.benefit: Designate a single vehicle, object, or creature in line of sight; gain extra damage dice for batteries (scaling every 2 batteries) and may designate one weapon or battery to make a single attack.
A number of vehicles equal to your class level in line of sight may each move a number of squares equal to their speed.
system.description: Move a number of allied vehicles equal to your class level.

system.benefit: Designate a single vehicle; allied gunners in line of sight gain +1 damage die.
system.description: Allied gunners gain +1 die damage against a designated target.

system.benefit: Once per encounter set a trap for a single vehicle in line of sight that activates on that vehicle's immediate move action.
system.description: Once per encounter, force a vehicle in line of sight to immediately move.

system.benefit: Gunners on your ship add half your heroic level (or half their HL) to damage and treat crew as one level higher; calculate capital ship Reflex Defense as your HL + 1/2 armor if higher.
system.description: Gunners add half heroic level to damage; calculate capital ship defenses using your heroic level.

system.benefit: When an ally or opponent in line of sight rolls a natural 1 on an attack, you may make an attack against a single target.
system.description: When an ally or enemy rolls a natural 1 on an attack, you may make an attack against a target.

system.benefit: If an enemy in line of sight moves an ally down the condition track, gain +2 attack against that enemy.
system.description: When an enemy moves an ally down the condition track, gain +2 attack against that enemy.

system.benefit: When you damage an opponent, you may move through that opponent’s square; attacks of opportunity apply as normal.
system.description: When you damage an opponent, you may move through their square.

system.benefit: When an enemy or ally within 20 squares is reduced to 0 hit points, you gain hit points equal to 5 + half your character level.
system.description: When any creature within range is reduced to 0 hit points, gain temporary hit points.

system.benefit: When an enemy you reduced to 0 hit points or the bottom of the condition track, your next attack deals +1 die of damage.
system.description: When you defeat an enemy, your next attack deals +1 die of damage.

system.benefit: Once per encounter forgo your extra move to attack; if you hit and damage with a melee attack, you switch places with the opponent.
system.description: Once per encounter, forgo an extra move to attack; if successful, switch places with the opponent.

system.benefit: Once per encounter forgo the extra swift to attack; if you hit and damage with a ranged attack, count your and an ally's attack as one for overcoming DR/SR/Threshold.
system.description: Once per encounter, forgo an extra swift action to combine your ranged attack with an ally's for damage resolution.

system.benefit: Forfeit the extra swift action to aim with one swift on your next turn.
system.description: Forgo an extra swift action to Aim as a swift action on your next turn.

system.benefit: Once per encounter forgo your extra move to add +5 to one Defense until the end of your next turn.
system.description: Once per encounter, forgo an extra move to gain +5 to one defense until the end of your next turn.

system.benefit: Once per encounter add half your class level to one Defense.
system.description: Once per encounter, add half your class level to one defense.

system.benefit: Once per round when an ally damages an opponent, you may take a swift action as a reaction.
system.description: Once per round, when an ally damages an opponent, you may take a swift action as a reaction.

system.benefit: If a non-area attack hits, you may choose to do half damage and force the target to lose its next move.
system.description: When hit by a non-area attack, you may choose to take half damage and the target loses its next move action.

system.benefit: Once per round when damaged, move 1 square as a reaction without provoking attacks of opportunity.
system.description: Once per round when damaged, move 1 square as a reaction without provoking attacks of opportunity.

system.benefit: When you jury-rig a vehicle, it regains hit points equal to your Mechanics check result.
system.description: When jury-rigging a vehicle, restore hit points equal to your Mechanics check.

system.benefit: Use Mechanics instead of Use Computer to improve computer access; you are considered trained.
system.description: Use Mechanics instead of Use Computer to improve access to systems.

system.benefit: For powered weapons you are using, gain +1 attack and +2 damage for the rest of the encounter.
system.description: Powered weapons you use gain +1 attack and +2 damage for the encounter.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, jury-rig an object that is not disabled.
system.description: Once per encounter, jury-rig an object that is not disabled.

system.benefit: If adjacent to an object that provides cover to your target, you may aim at that target as a move action.
system.description: If adjacent to an object that provides cover to the target, you may Aim at the target as a move action.

system.benefit: Enemies treat your Safe Zone as difficult terrain.
system.description: Enemies treat your safe zone as difficult terrain.

swse.description: When flat-footed, use your Will Defense instead

system.benefit: One ally in line of sight who has cover is treated as having improved cover while they still have cover.
system.description: One ally in line of sight who has cover is treated as having improved cover as long as they retain cover.

system.benefit: When you move one adjacent ally, that ally moves and ends adjacent to you; cannot exceed the ally’s movement.
system.description: When you move, one adjacent ally may move with you and remain adjacent, not exceeding the ally's speed.

system.benefit: Any ally who starts in your Safe Zone and then exits it gains +2 attack.
system.description: Allies who start in your safe zone and then leave it gain +2 attack.

system.benefit: Enemies firing into your Safe Zone take a -2 penalty to attack.
system.description: Enemies firing into your safe zone take –2 attack.

system.benefit: Dismiss your Safe Zone; allies who were in your old Safe Zone gain +2 speed.
system.description: Dismiss your safe zone; allies who were in it gain +2 speed.

system.benefit: Once per turn one ally in line of sight may move its speed as a reaction; if a target makes an attack of opportunity against that ally you may make an attack of opportunity against that target.
system.description: Once per turn, an ally in line of sight may move its speed as a reaction; if an enemy makes an attack of opportunity against that ally, you may make an attack of opportunity against that enemy.

system.benefit: Create a 4×4 square Safe Zone; an ally that starts its turn in the Safe Zone gains +2 Fortitude and +2 Will Defense; Safe Zones cannot overlap another Safe Zone.
system.description: Create a 4×4 square safe zone; allies starting in it gain +2 Fortitude and Will Defense. Safe zones cannot overlap.

system.benefit: Any ally who uses Second Wind in your Safe Zone regains additional hit points equal to your class level.
system.description: Allies who use Second Wind in your safe zone gain additional hit points equal to your class level.

system.benefit: Perform coup de grace as a move action; if you kill with a coup de grace all allies in line of sight gain +2 attack for the encounter.
system.description: Perform a coup de grace as a move action; if you kill with it, allies in line of sight gain +2 attack for the encounter.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, all allies within 6 squares heal hit points equal to your heroic level.
system.description: Once per encounter, allies within 6 squares regain hit points equal to your heroic level.

system.benefit: Make a single melee attack within reach; if it hits deal no damage but force the target to move or withdraw next turn.
Once per round, if you or an ally within 6 squares damage a target, allies in line of sight deal additional damage equal to half your class level to that target.
system.description: Once per round, when you or an ally damages a target, allies in line of sight gain bonus damage equal to half your class level.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, Deception or Persuasion vs Will in line of sight forces the target to withdraw or suffer -1 attack until end of encounter.
system.description: Once per encounter, Persuasion or Deception vs Will Defense; target must withdraw or take –1 attack until the end of the encounter.

system.benefit: All opponents within 6 squares take -1 attack.
system.description: Opponents within 6 squares take –1 attack.

system.benefit: If you or allies within 6 squares reduce an enemy to 0 hp, you may instead render them stunned.
system.description: When you or allies reduce an enemy to 0 hit points, you may instead treat them as stunned.

swse.description: Use Persuasion checks instead of combat attacks for capture/knockout

system.prerequisites: Dual Weapon Mastery I, Running Attack
system.benefit: Once per encounter with two pistols, move twice your speed and make attacks with each pistol.
system.description: Once per encounter, while wielding two pistols, move up to twice your speed and make one attack with each.

system.benefit: If flanked and wielding two pistols, make flanking attacks as a standard action instead of a full; must attack two targets.
system.description: If flanked while wielding two pistols, attack both flankers as a standard action instead of a full action.

system.benefit: With two pistols, even if you miss, deal damage equal to half your heroic level.
system.description: When attacking with two pistols, even if you miss, deal damage equal to half your heroic level.

system.benefit: With two pistols, attack as if autofire even without autofire, applying autofire penalties.
system.description: When wielding two pistols, you may attack as if using autofire; autofire penalties apply.

system.prerequisites: Dual Weapon Mastery I, Rapid Shot
system.benefit: With two pistols and Rapid Shot, gain +2 damage.
system.description: When using Rapid Shot with two pistols, gain +2 damage.

system.benefit: Treat armor proficiency as Armor Proficiency (heavy).
system.description: You are treated as having Armor Proficiency (heavy).

swse.description: Your armor proficiency is treated as heavy armor proficiency

system.benefit: Attract nonheroic minions with levels equal to 3/4 your level; may have multiple minions.
system.description: Attract a nonheroic character of three-quarters your level; may be taken multiple times.

system.prerequisites: Dual Weapon I, Weapon Proficiency (advanced), Weapon Proficiency (pistols)
system.benefit: With a single attack using an advanced melee weapon, make a free pistol attack.
system.description: When attacking with an advanced melee weapon, make a free pistol attack.

system.benefit: Treat the advanced melee weapon as if you were holding it two-handed for Strength bonus purposes (double Str bonus).
system.description: Treat the advanced melee weapon as two-handed for Strength damage.

system.benefit: When attacking with both weapons, you may make a full attack as a standard action.
system.description: You may make a full attack as a standard action when attacking with both weapons.

system.benefit: Ignore cover for character-scale ranged attacks when aboard a starship or space station.
system.description: Ignore cover with character-scale ranged attacks aboard starships or space stations.

system.benefit: With ion weapons, gain +1 attack and +1 damage die.
system.description: Gain +1 attack and +1 die damage with ion weapons.

system.benefit: Reduce multiattack penalties by 2 for advanced melee weapons.
system.description: Reduce the attack penalty for multiple attacks with advanced melee weapons by 2.

system.benefit: Instead of destroying a vehicle, you can disable it so it cannot move.
system.description: Instead of destroying a vehicle, you may disable it so it cannot move.

system.benefit: Automatically locate a black market merchant.
system.description: You can automatically locate a black market merchant.

system.benefit: All gear you purchase has +50% hit points, +5 Damage Reduction, and you gain +2 on Mechanics checks with those objects.
system.description: All gear you purchase gains +50% hit points, +5 damage reduction, and +2 on Mechanics checks made with it.

system.benefit: When using Repair or Mechanics, retemplates/apps/store an additional +1d8 hit points.
system.description: When using Repair or Mechanics with Aid Another, restore +1d8 hit points.

system.benefit: Obtain modified items without increasing their base value.
system.description: You may obtain modified items without increasing their base value.

system.benefit: +5 equipment bonus to an ally's untrained check and treat the ally as trained (not for Use the Force).
system.description: Grant an ally a +5 equipment bonus on an untrained skill check and treat them as trained (except Use the Force).

system.benefit: When using Ward, add one-half your armor bonuses to the ally’s Reflex Defense.
system.description: When using Ward, add half your armor bonus to the ally's Reflex Defense.

system.benefit: Take any or all damage that would target an adjacent ally; cannot use again until the end of your next turn.
system.description: Take any or all damage for an adjacent ally; remaining damage applies to the original target.

system.benefit: Whenever you begin your turn adjacent to the Ward target, gain hit points equal to your character level.
system.description: When you begin your turn adjacent to a warded ally, gain hit points equal to your character level.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, when an adjacent ally is reduced to 0 hp or below threshold, you may move your speed to become adjacent and take the damage instead.
system.description: Once per encounter, move your speed to become adjacent to an ally reduced to 0 hit points and take the damage instead.

system.benefit: Allow one ally within 6 squares to move their speed to become adjacent to you without provoking attacks of opportunity.
system.description: Allow one ally within 6 squares to move its speed to be adjacent to you without provoking attacks of opportunity.

system.benefit: If you take damage for an ally, gain Damage Reduction equal to your class level until the end of your next turn.
system.description: When you take damage for an ally, gain damage reduction equal to your class level until the end of your next turn.

system.benefit: If you take damage for an ally, your damage threshold is +5.
system.description: When you take damage for an ally, your damage threshold increases by 5.

system.benefit: Provide soft cover to one adjacent ally.
system.description: Provide soft cover to one adjacent ally.

system.benefit: One enemy in line of sight loses all insight and morale bonuses on attacks and cannot be aided until the end of your next turn.
system.description: An enemy loses insight and morale bonuses and cannot be aided.

system.benefit: When an ally in line of sight takes Second Wind, all enemies within 2 squares lose Dexterity to Defense until the end of your next turn.
system.description: Enemies lose Dexterity to Defense when an ally uses Second Wind.

system.benefit: Once per turn, when an ally in line of sight is missed by a ranged attack, compare the missed attack against an adjacent enemy to see if it hits.
system.description: A missed ranged attack may hit an adjacent enemy instead.

system.benefit: Once per turn, when an enemy is reduced to 0 hp or moves down the condition track, one ally in line of sight may take Second Wind as a free action and adds hit points equal to their character level.
system.description: Once per round, when an ally damages an opponent, you may take a swift action as a reaction.

system.benefit: When an enemy in line of sight aids another, grant +2 attack to one ally in line of sight and the enemy provides no benefit.
system.description: When an enemy aids another, you grant bonuses to an ally instead.

system.benefit: Make Persuasion vs Will Defense of one enemy in line of sight to compel them to attack a target you choose; +5 bonus if they are higher level.
system.description: Force an enemy to attack a target of your choosing.

system.benefit: If Recruit Enemy succeeds, allies gain +2 attack until end of encounter.
system.description: After successfully recruiting an enemy, allies gain +2 attack until the end of the encounter.

system.benefit: If a recruited enemy is at 0 hp or bottom of track, you and all allies in line of sight gain hit points equal to 10 + your class level.
system.description: When a recruited enemy is defeated, allies gain hit points.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, when you deal damage equal to the target’s current hp and threshold, make Persuasion vs Will Defense; on success deal half damage, move target -1 on track, and the target becomes friendly (higher-level targets get +5 on Will).
system.description: Once per encounter, convert a damaged enemy into an ally through Persuasion.

system.benefit: A recruited enemy may immediately use Second Wind as a reaction.
You can use Recruit Enemy when you or an ally would deal killing damage, not only when you deal it.
system.description: You or allies may recruit an enemy instead of killing them.

system.benefit: If you are missed by an attack, gain +2 on your next attack before the end of your next turn.
system.description: If you are missed by an attack, gain +2 on your next attack before the end of your next turn.

system.benefit: Allies take no or half damage from your area attack.
system.description: Allies take no or half damage from your area attacks.

system.benefit: Once per turn, make Deception vs Will Defense against one enemy within 12 squares and line of sight; the target must move half its speed toward you avoiding hazards (movement stops if a hazard cannot be avoided); does not provoke attacks of opportunity.
system.description: Force an enemy to move toward you using Deception vs Will Defense.

system.benefit: Once per turn, if you are damaged by an attack of opportunity, make a single attack against a target.
system.description: Once per turn, if damaged, make a single attack against a target.

system.benefit: Make an Initiative check against an enemy in line of sight; the loser is flat-footed against the other.
system.description: Opposed Initiative check; the loser is flat-footed against the winner.

system.benefit: Each follower gains the Point Blank Shot feat.
system.description: Followers gain the Point Blank Shot feat.

system.prerequisites: Base attack bonus +5, Reconnaissance Team Leader
system.benefit: One of your followers gains +2 speed.
system.description: One follower gains increased speed.

system.benefit: When you attack: grant +2 ranged attack to each follower; or +1 Stealth to each; or +1 Perception to each if armed with ranged weapons.
system.description: Followers gain bonuses to attacks, Stealth, or Perception when you attack.

system.benefit: Gain a follower trained in Perception and Stealth (max 3 times); follower can make Stealth checks with you.
system.description: Gain a follower trained in Perception and Stealth; may be taken multiple times.

system.benefit: If you hit an opponent that has not yet acted, add +2 dice of damage.
Once per encounter, add +1 damage die to a grenade or explosive.
system.description: Once per encounter, deal +1 die of damage with a grenade or explosive.

system.benefit: Retrieve and load an energy cell or power pack as a single swift action.
system.description: Retrieve and reload an energy cell or power pack as a single swift action.

system.benefit: If you begin your turn adjacent to an ally, gain hit points equal to your heroic level.
system.description: If you begin your turn adjacent to an ally, gain hit points equal to your heroic level.

system.benefit: If within 10 squares of an ally, gain +2 Damage Reduction.
system.description: While within 10 squares of an ally, gain +2 damage reduction.

system.benefit: When using a ranged weapon as a melee weapon, gain +2 melee attack.
system.description: When using a ranged weapon as a melee weapon, gain +2 melee attack.

system.benefit: Expend a charge to make an attack as a flamethrower; cannot use while flying.
Increase fly speed by 2 squares.
Activate a jet pack as a free action; no Pilot check required to land.
Once per encounter, when an enemy moves adjacent, expend one charge to fly, move your speed, or withdraw.
Once per encounter, make an attack of opportunity against an opponent that withdraws from an ally in point-blank range.
system.description: Once per encounter, make an attack of opportunity against a withdrawing opponent.

system.benefit: When an opponent withdraws, that opponent is considered flat-footed against you until the end of your next turn.
system.description: Opponents withdrawing from you are flat-footed until the end of your next turn.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, make an attack of opportunity against an opponent in point-blank range if they provoke an attack of opportunity from an ally.
system.description: Once per encounter, make an attack of opportunity against an enemy in point-blank range.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, designate an opponent you just damaged; that opponent cannot make attacks of opportunity until your next turn.
system.description: Once per encounter, a damaged opponent cannot make attacks of opportunity.

system.benefit: Once per encounter, after you damage an opponent, you may move your speed.
system.description: Once per encounter, after dealing damage you may move your speed.

system.benefit: DC 20 Mechanics vs an item; on success all items of that type within 12 squares cease functioning; affects one device at a time.
system.description: With a DC 20 Mechanics check, disable all items of one type within 12 squares; only one jammer may be active at a time.

system.benefit: Mechanics vs a droid’s Will Defense within 6 squares; on success the droid can take only swift actions; affects one droid at a time.
system.description: Make a Mechanics check vs a droid's Will Defense; if successful, the droid may take only swift actions.

system.benefit: Increase blast radius by 1 square.
system.description: Increase the blast radius of a shaped explosion by 1 square.

system.benefit: Place mines as a standard action instead of a full-round action.
system.description: You may place a mine as a standard action instead of a full-round action.

system.benefit: Create blasts in a line or cone: length = 2× radius; cone length = 3× blast radius; origin from explosive’s square.
system.description: Explosions may be shaped into a line or cone originating from the explosive's square.

system.benefit: Set a detonator as a swift action; explosives do not detonate on a failure by 10 or more.
system.description: Set a detonator as a swift action; explosives do not detonate if you fail by 10 or more.

system.benefit: Ignore all concealment for 1 minute.
system.description: Ignore all concealment for 1 minute.

system.benefit: Use the Force replaces Perception to avoid surprise, notice enemies, and sense deception or influence; you are considered trained.
system.description: Use the Force replaces Perception to avoid surprise and detect deception.

system.benefit: Use the Force instead of Pilot; you are considered trained in Pilot.
system.description: Use the Force instead of Pilot and are considered trained in Pilot.

system.benefit: When activating a starship maneuver, reroll the Pilot check.
system.description: When activating a starship maneuver, reroll the Pilot check.

system.benefit: Reroll Initiative; on a natural 20, regain a Force Point.
system.description: You may reroll Initiative checks; on a natural 20, regain a Force Point.

system.benefit: Use the Force vs Will to detect Force sensitivity, number of powers known, and Force Points.
system.description: Use the Force to detect Force sensitivity, powers known, and Force Points.

system.benefit: Add your Charisma bonus to Perception checks.
system.description: Add your Charisma modifier to Perception checks.

system.benefit: Use the Force replaces Use Computer for astrogation checks.
system.description: Use the Force instead of Use Computer for astrogation.

system.benefit: Expend farseeing from your suite; before end of your next turn, force an attacker to reroll an attack and keep the second result.
system.description: Force an attacker to reroll an attack and keep the second result.

system.benefit: Target an object you hold; read impressions up to 5 years per level.
system.description: Read impressions from an object you hold, seeing events from the past.

system.benefit: Gain low-light vision for 1 minute or the rest of the encounter, whichever is longer.
system.description: Gain low-light vision for 1 minute or the encounter.

system.benefit: See into the past or future with farseeing, up to one year per your level.
system.description: See possible past or future events using Farseeing.

system.benefit: Modify biotech in half the usual time and cost; may take 10 on Mechanics checks for biotech work.
system.description: Biotech modifications take half the normal time and cost; you may take 10 on Mechanics checks.

system.benefit: Mend a damaged or disabled biotech device as a standard action instead of a full action.
system.description: You may mend damaged or disabled biotech as a standard action.

system.benefit: Reroll Treat Injury checks to repair or modify biotech devices.
system.description: Reroll Treat Injury checks made to repair or modify biotech.

system.benefit: When you temporarily mend biotech using Treat Injury, move +4 up the condition track and only move -3 down at the end of the encounter.
system.description: Temporarily mended biotech moves +4 up the condition track and only –3 at the end of the encounter.

system.benefit: When installing an implant you count as having the Biotech Surgery feat for attack bonus calculations (attack bonus is halved).
system.description: When installing an implant, the attack bonus penalty is halved.

system.benefit: Your Force Powers carries the taint of The Dark Side more so than even other Dark Side users. When you damage a single opponent with one of your Force Powers, that target also takes 2d6 points of Force damage at the beginning of its next turn, before taking any Actions.
system.description: Your Force Powers carries the taint of The Dark Side more so than even other Dark Side users. When you damage a single opponent with one of your Force Powers, that target also takes 2d6 points of Force damage at the beginning of its next turn, before taking any Actions.

abilityMeta.notes: After damaging one opponent with a Force Power, mark it to take 2d6 Force damage at the beginning of its next turn.

combatActions.name: Affliction
combatActions.label: Affliction
combatActions.description: Your Force Powers carries the taint of The Dark Side more so than even other Dark Side users. When you damage a single opponent with one of your Force Powers, that target also takes 2d6 points of Force damage at the beginning of its next turn, before taking any Actions.
combatActions.notes: After damaging one opponent with a Force Power, mark it to take 2d6 Force damage at the beginning of its next turn.

system.benefit: You can spend a Force Point to heal wounds by drawing life energy from another creature within 6 squares of you. Using this ability is a Standard Action, and you must succeed on a ranged attack roll. If the attack equals or exceeds the target's Fortitude Defense, you deal 1d6 points of damage per Class Level to the target, and you heal an equal amount of damage. system.benefit:
Your knowledge of Sith Alchemy allows you to imbue certain objects with the power of the Dark Side. You can perform any of the following Alchemical Transformations, though each time you do so, increase your Dark Side Score by 1.

Create Sith Amulet: You can create a Sith Amulet. The creation of a Sith Amulet requires 25,000 credits' worth of gems and other raw materials, and the creation process takes 1 week (this work can be spread out over several sessions and need not be consecutive). At the end of the process, you must spend a Force Point to complete the Sith Amulet, after which point it functions exactly as a Sith Amulet.

Create Sith Armor: You can transform a suit of normal Battle Armor into the Dark Armor worn by Sith Lords. You must have a suit of Battle Armor to transform into the Dark Armor (Light Battle Armor becomes Light Dark Armor, Battle Armor becomes Dark Armor, and Heavy Battle Armor becomes Heavy Dark Armor). The transformation process takes 1 day for Light Dark Armor, 2 days for Dark Armor, and 3 days for Heavy Dark Armor. You must spend a Force Point at the end of the creation process to complete the Armor's transformation.

Create Sith Talisman: You can spend a Force Point to imbue a portable object with the Dark Side, creating a Sith Talisman that provides offensive strength to a Force Power. Creating the Sith Talisman takes a Full-Round Action. While you wear or carry the Sith Talisman on your person, you add 1d6 to your damage with Force Powers. You increase your Dark Side Score by 1 when you first wear or carry a Sith Talisman. You can have only one Sith Talisman active at any given time, and if it is destroyed, you cannot create another one for 24 hours.

Create Sith Weapon: You can alchemically treat a Simple Weapon (Melee) or an Advanced Melee Weapon, turning it into a Sith Weapon. You must spend a Force Point and spend 1 hour imbuing the Weapon with the properties of a Sith Alchemical Weapon (see also: Sith Alchemical Weapon Template). A Lightsaber does not ignore the Sith Weapon's DR, and characters who are proficient in the Weapon's use can treat it as a Lightsaber for the purposes of the Block, Deflect, and Redirect Shot Talents (and any Talents that have these Talents as a prerequisite). Additionally, as a Swift Action, the wielder of a Sith Weapon can spend a Force Point to gain a bonus equal to their Dark Side Score to the damage of their next attack made with the Weapon before the end of the encounter. This increases the wielder's Dark Side Score by 1.

system.benefit: Create Sith Amulets, Sith Armor, Sith Talismans, or Sith Weapons per Sith Alchemy rules.

You can modify an object with Sith Alchemy so that it gains a specific trait. Specific traits are listed in the Sith Alchemy Table, below. You can only perform one modification at a time. Unless otherwise noted, you cannot grant more than one benefit to a single object, and you cannot apply the same benefit more than once. You must spend a Force Point and devote 1 hour of uninterrupted work to apply a trait to the relevant object, and when you do so, you increase your Dark Side Score by 1.

Sith Alchemy Traits:

Dark Armor Trait - Cortosis Weave: Automatically deactivates any Lightsaber that strikes the wearer.

Dark Armor Trait - Dark Side Energy: Grants the wearer a +2 Equipment bonus on Use the Force checks made to activate Force Powers with the [Dark Side] descriptor.

Dark Armor Trait - Dark Side Stealth: Grants the wearer a +2 Equipment bonus on Use the Force checks made to avoid detection.

Dark Armor Trait - Imposing Form: Grants the wearer a +2 Equipment bonus on Persuasion checks made to Intimidate.

Sith Weapon Trait - Jagged Weapon: Any time the Sith Weapon damages a living creature, that creature takes 1d4 points of damage at the start of its next turn.

Sith Weapon Trait - Master's Weapon: The Sith Weapon grants a +2 Equipment bonus on Use the Force checks to use the Block and Deflect Talents.

Sith Weapon Trait - Vile Weapon: The Sith Weapon is always considered to be laced with Sith Poison.

Sith Abomination Trait - Damage Reduction: The Sith Abomination gains Damage Reduction 5, or increases its existing DR by +5.

Sith Abomination Trait - Iron Will: The Sith Abomination gains a +2 Equipment bonus to its Will Defense.

Sith Abomination Trait - Vile Natural Weapons: The Sith Abomination's Natural Weapons are laced with Sith Poison.

system.benefit:
When you touch a living creature with an Intelligence score of 3 or higher, you can use The Force to probe its mind for secrets. You must be adjacent to the target, and using the Mind Probe is a Full-Round Action. If the target is unwilling, you must succeed on a Use the Force check, equaling or exceeding the target's Will Defense. This ability otherwise functions exactly as the Gather Information skill's Learn News and Rumors, Learn Secret Information, and Locate Individual applications.

Your Use the Force check must still exceed the base Gather Information skill DCs in order to retrieve the information you seek, but you need not pay anything in bribes, and you retrieve the information as a part of the Full-Round Action. Failing the Skill Check by 5 or more does not cause someone to notice that you are seeking the information.

description.value:
You are particularly in tune with The Living Force. When you successfully use Search Your Feelings, you gain a Force Point that must be used before the end of the encounter.

If you use this Force Point in a manner that would end in unfavorable results (per Search Your Feelings), you raise your Dark Side Score by 1. If you use the Force Point in an action that would normally raise your Dark Side Score, you raise your Dark Side Score by 2 instead.

description.value:
When using the Telepathy ability of Use the Force, you reduce the DC of the Use the Force check by half, as do those Force-users for whom you are a willing telepathic recipient. When attempting to contact an unwilling target, you can reroll and take the better result.

By spending a Force Point, you can simultaneously contact a number of targets equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum two) with a single Use the Force check.
